name: Wagtail Django CMS REST API
what_it_does: Open-source Django-based CMS with REST API — enabling Python development teams to build content-managed websites and headless backends with a flexible StreamField content model, REST API for headless delivery, image processing, document management, multi-site support, and a beautiful editing interface, deployed on any Python/Django hosting.
use_cases: Delivering headless content via Wagtail REST API for decoupled frontend applications
Managing Wagtail pages and snippets via API for content migration automation
Querying Wagtail image renditions via API for responsive image delivery
Integrating Wagtail with Django applications via internal API for CMS-backed Python apps
Building multi-site content networks via Wagtail API for enterprise publishing operations
not_for: Teams without Python/Django expertise (Wagtail is a Django application)
Non-technical teams needing managed CMS without server management
Teams wanting a database-free flat-file CMS (Wagtail uses PostgreSQL/MySQL)
best_when: A Python/Django development team needs a production-grade CMS with a beautiful editing interface — backed by Django's security, PostgreSQL performance, and a rich StreamField content model for complex page structures.
avoid_when: You don't have Python/Django expertise, need managed SaaS, or are building non-Django applications.

auth.notes: Wagtail API v2 is read-only by default (no auth required for public content). Write API via Wagtail REST Framework integration or custom Django views. Django REST Framework token auth for protected endpoints. No built-in OAuth2 — use Django OAuth Toolkit if needed. Webhook via Wagtail hooks system (Python hooks, not HTTP webhooks natively). Wagtail API read-only by design — write operations via Django admin or custom views.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free and open source (BSD license)
pricing.paid_tiers: Wagtail Space (managed cloud) pricing; Torchbox consulting available
pricing.notes: Open source project. Created by Torchbox (UK digital agency) in 2014. BSD license. GitHub: wagtail/wagtail (17,000+ stars). Used by NASA, Google, UK Government (GOV.UK), Mozilla, Coca-Cola, World Wildlife Fund. Python 3 + Django framework. StreamField: flexible content block model. Headless capability via API v2. Image processing built-in (Pillow). Multi-site, i18n, accessibility-first design. Torchbox provides commercial support. Wagtail Space Cloud is managed hosting. Very popular in UK public sector and nonprofit sector.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Read-only REST API v2 by default. No authentication required for public content. Write operations require Django views or DRF endpoints. No native OAuth2. Webhook via Python hooks (not HTTP). Good documentation. Large community. Open source. Python/Django expertise required. Self-hosted — agents need network access.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All content reads fully idempotent by page ID or slug. Image rendition reads idempotent. Document reads idempotent. API is read-only by default — no write mutations to worry about.
known_agent_gotchas: API v2 is READ-ONLY by default — write operations require custom Django views
No native HTTP webhooks — uses Django signal hooks (Python code, not URL webhooks)
StreamField content requires understanding Wagtail's block model for parsing
Self-hosted — agents need network access to the Django server

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS depends on server config. User-controlled data residency. API v2 read-only by default — no write attack surface. Django security model (CSRF, XSS protection). DRF token auth for protected endpoints. Django's security track record is strong. Open source — auditable. Used by UK Government (security requirements). GDPR compliance user's responsibility. Large open source community ensures rapid security patching.
